license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7krhkv6
title: Antibiotic Stocks
author: Alba Balletbó

steps_raw.instruction: Unless otherwise indicated, the antibiotic powder can be dissolved in dH 2 0. Addgene recommends making 1000X stock solutions and storing aliquots at -20ºC. %s %s 'Note' : All are dissolved in MQ except Chloramphenicol, which is dissolved in ethanol. All antibiotic mixes were filter-sterilized before use.
To use, dilute your antibiotic into your LB medium at 1:1,000. For example, to make 100 mL of LB/ampicillin growth media, add 100 μL of a 100 mg/mL ampicillin stock (1000X stock) to 100 mL of LB.
Weigh the antibiotic powders needed for 10 mL of Recommended Stock Concentration Solution.
Filter sterilize and aliquot into 1.5 mL tubes.
Add the weighed powder to the MQ or ethanol solution. Mix/vortex vigorously until the powder is completely dissolved.
